verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase canonically localized to the cytoplasm via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation (inner-leaflet anchoring). However, LYN has documented surface pools in specific contexts: (1) mast cell degranulation relocates LYN to the PM as part of FcεRI signaling; (2) B-cell activation induces LYN surface translocation via BCR engagement; (3) tumor cells display LYN on the outer leaflet (cancer ecto-kinase pool). The baseline compartment is cytoplasmic / inner-leaflet, but the protein reaches the outer face under activation and oncogenic state, qualifying as cell_state_induced.
